{"step_by_step_thinking": "Platelets contain dense granules that store serotonin. When platelets are activated, they degranulate and release serotonin into the bloodstream. This is explicitly stated in multiple documents: Document [16] mentions that dense granules contain serotonin, and Document [20] confirms that dense granules store serotonin. Document [15] and Document [19] describe serotonin being released during platelet activation and functioning as a mediator of platelet aggregation and vasoconstriction. The serotonin release assay (SRA), used to diagnose heparin-induced thrombocytopenia, relies on the release of serotonin from platelets when activated by patient serum and heparin, confirming that platelets release serotonin. Therefore, platelets not only store serotonin but also transport and release it upon activation. This indicates that serotonin is transported (stored and released) by platelets.", "answer_choice": "A"}
